figures_detected.description: Continuity check being performed on electromagnetic clutch slip ring with a tester
Insulation check being performed between slip ring and electromagnetic clutch main unit bracket with a tester
Electromagnetic clutch slip ring placed flat side down on clean surface for condition check
Assembly diagram showing A spline shaft, B spline shaft, and O-ring locations for electromagnetic clutch installation

content_blocks.text: Check the resistance between the electromagnetic clutch slip rings with a tester. Check that the resistance is within the following range.

items.label: Standard value
items.condition: Resistance between electromagnetic clutch slip rings

content_blocks.text: If there is no continuity or the resistance value is extremely low, replace the electromagnetic clutch assembly.
Do not press the tester probe with excessive force against the slip ring and do not damage the outside surface.
Check the continuity between the slip ring and electromagnetic clutch main unit bracket (aluminium) with a tester. Replace the electromagnetic clutch if continuity exists.
Do not press the tester probe with excessive force against the slip ring and do not damage the outside surface.
Wipe off any dust, debris or oil that adheres to the outside of the slip ring with thinner or solvent. Check the slip ring for damage.
Performing the inspection on a flat, clean surface free from dust and oil. Make sure that the slip ring side faces up.
Perform the following procedures to install the electromagnetic clutch in the main unit.

steps.text: Rotate the oil pump shaft (B spline shaft) and check that the splines are engaged correctly.
Insert the electromagnetic clutch main unit and check that it is engaged correctly with both the A and B spline shafts.
Rotate the electromagnetic clutch slightly if it is difficult to insert, because the splines engage at two locations.

content_blocks.text: (1) Do not apply excessive force to the A and B spline shafts.

(2) If the electromagnetic clutch is inserted with excessive force, it may interfere at the location indicated by an asterisk (*). This may cause damage to the clutch assembly and internal components.
(3) If the electromagnetic clutch is removed and installed, always replace the O-rings on the shaft with new parts.
